title: Drive Compose from observable state
description: Back composables with State/StateFlow so changing the value triggers recomposition, rather than mutating views imperatively.
body: Use when building Jetpack Compose UI. Do not store changing UI data in a plain var and expect the screen to update, and do not try to imperatively mutate composables. Actions: hold transient UI state in remember { mutableStateOf(...) }, collect ViewModel StateFlow with collectAsStateWithLifecycle, and let Compose recompose when the observed state changes; hoist state to the lowest common owner. Validation signals include the UI updating automatically on a state change and no manual refresh calls. Risk boundary: mutating a non-State variable leaves the UI stale with no error, while reading state in the wrong scope causes missed or excessive recomposition — the observability of the state is what makes Compose update at all.
